reference: Dear Madam, I am thirty-four years old and I have been a diabetic since the age of twenty-five. I am on tablets. I take Glimsev one-zero-one, Glysipage one-one-one, and Voglitor one-zero-one. I have a nine-year-old daughter; I conceived her during the first cycle of intrauterine insemination at GG Hospital in Chennai. I was using a Copper T, which I removed in two thousand and eight. During the intrauterine insemination process, there was an ectopic pregnancy in the right tube, and it was sterilized at the time of the lower segment cesarean section. Even though we are not using any birth control since removing the Copper T, I am unable to conceive. My periods are regular. What are the chances of conceiving naturally?
text: Dear Madam, I am 34 years old, and I have been a diabetic since the age of 25. I am on tablets. I take Glymsev 101, Glycipage 111, and Voglator 101. I have a 9-year-old daughter, who was during the first cycle of intrauterine insemination at Gigi Hospital in Chiang Mai. I was using a copper tube, which I removed in 2008. During the intrauterine insemination process, there was an ectopic pregnancy in the right tube, and it was sterilized at the time of the lower segment cesarean section. Even though we are not using any birth control since removing the copper tube, I am unable to conceive. My periods are regular. What are the chances of conceiving naturally?
